action.reasoning: Observation: MLP v2 got test IC=0.011361 (best so far). GRU got val IC=0.0247 but hasn't been evaluated. Models overfit quickly (best at epoch 3-4). Analysis: The large gap between val IC (~0.027) and test IC (~0.011) suggests overfitting/distribution shift. LightGBM models got near-zero test IC. Let me try: 1) A better LightGBM with stronger regularization and more trees, 2) Linear model (Ridge) as robust baseline, 3) Ensemble. The key insight: simpler models may generalize better for financial data. Also, I should try training with MSE loss which is more stable than IC loss. Decision: Train a new approach - use LightGBM with proper hyperparameters (lambda_l1=0.1, lambda_l2=0.1, min_data_in_leaf=50, num_leaves=64, n_estimators=500, learning_rate=0.05) and also try a linear model. Also try an ensemble of MLP + LightGBM predictions.
